comment: The Hoava dialect prefers to use sa for a general pronoun and isa emphatically or anaphorically. The Kusaghe dialect uses isa more often as a general pronoun, but also use sa.

translation: Kusaghe dialect uses ria karu (cf Davis 2003:46-47). The related definite article system also shows the same bias.
